mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals synchronized consumer and business demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO month-over-month changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 2.5%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 2.5% over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of months over a 7-year rolling test period.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity that flow through to broader corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive housing starts changes correlate with negative 60-day returns in >50% of months over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising GDP indicates inventory drawdowns that precede earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.
